deduplication.rule: Retain distinct causal economics. Remove only true sign, horizon, conditioning, operational-identity, or index/owner variants; do not target a fixed count.

removals.reason: Same FOMC scheduled-date mechanism and daily next-session horizon; wording does not add a causal channel.
CPI pre-release negative-control and horizon variant of the retained CPI release repricing mechanism.
Employment Situation pre-release negative-control and horizon variant of the retained labor-release repricing mechanism.
Same monthly listed-option expiration economics; the Lane B card carries the stricter rulespan identity.
Same quarterly simultaneous-expiration economics; the Lane B card carries the stricter cross-product rulespan identity.
Index-owner variant of benchmark-tracking recomposition demand; S&P versus Russell is not independent economics.
Intraday horizon and cross-asset measurement variant of CPI release repricing.
Intraday horizon and cross-asset measurement variant of Employment Situation repricing.
Intraday horizon variant of Treasury auction inventory economics.
Risk-factor and litigation vocabulary is a conditioned subfield of filing-text change.
Industry neutralization and a one-week horizon condition the same short-term residual liquidity reversal.
VIX state is a conditioning rule on short-term liquidity-provision reversal.
Deletion is the sign/event-direction variant of benchmark-tracking forced demand around index recomposition.
Constituent-level inclusion implementation and index-owner variant of benchmark-tracking forced demand around index recomposition.
Annual implementation horizon and owner variant of Russell benchmark recomposition.
Same seasoned-equity-offering long-only exclusion economics and event type.
Abnormal-volume conditioning of 52-week-high anchoring.
Conjunction of separately retained earnings-innovation and revenue-innovation mechanisms.
Abnormal-volume conditioning of earnings underreaction.
Opposite-sign negative-control direction for PEAD.
Item 2.02 is an operational event-identity route, not separate economic mechanism.
Day-of-week attention conditioning of earnings underreaction.
Session-timing conditioning of earnings-announcement processing.
Reporting-owner variant of informative insider purchases.
Reporting-owner variant of informative insider purchases.
Plan-flag conditioning of informative insider purchases.

restored_or_explicitly_preserved.independence_reason: Industry common-cash-flow and information diffusion is independent from salient 52-week-high anchoring.
Factor-residual firm-specific diffusion is independent from industry momentum and 52-week-high anchoring.
Option skew measures downside-insurance asymmetry, not variance level compensation.
IV term slope measures the horizon of uncertainty, not skew or implied-minus-realized variance.
Large market opening gaps test continuation versus temporary pressure, distinct from generic overnight return decomposition.
ETF-versus-futures venue discovery differs from cross-asset overnight lead-lag.
Retail attention and opening-imbalance reversal differs from overnight clientele continuation.
Persistent open-session clientele continuation differs from opening liquidity reversal.
Opposing open and intraday clienteles form a joint decomposition distinct from either component alone.
Firm-specific residual overnight information differs from broad gap continuation or reversal.
Scheduled event-period risk and attention premium differs from post-announcement underreaction to surprise sign.
Disclosure delay and information decay differs from multiple-insider purchase clustering.
Aggregate scheduled-announcement risk compensation differs from indicator-specific CPI or labor repricing.
Information accumulation during closure differs from pre-holiday liquidity and inventory adjustment.

independence_audit.conclusion: Distinct economics remain separate even when data routes, instruments, or dates overlap.

source_binding_contract.requirement: Every retained card carries exactly one source_class. Classification follows the cited paper or primary source already bound in this outcome-blind atlas; mechanism mismatch is explicit and operational documentation alone is not mechanism evidence.

definitions.EXACT_MECHANISM_SOURCE: Cited source studies substantially the same signal direction, event timing, and economic channel.
definitions.ECONOMIC_PRIOR_ONLY: Cited source supports the economic state or causal channel but not the frozen tradable signal.
definitions.OPERATIONAL_ROUTE_ONLY: Documentation supports only data or market mechanics.
definitions.SOURCE_GAP: No credible source currently binds the proposed mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: NEXT_SESSION_RESPONSE: FOMC policy-announcement date anchoring for a distinct next-session response hypothesis without an intraday timestamp claim; the currently bound pre-announcement paper is economic-prior context only.
cards.primary_original_source: Lucca and Moench, The Pre-FOMC Announcement Drift, New York Fed Staff Report 512: https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/staff_reports/sr512.html
cards.universe: SPY daily session identity only; no prices accessed
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Official Statement release date; next accepted XNYS session open to the same session close, subject to separate preregistration.
required_data: mechanism-specific direct source for a next-session response
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: One event session per qualified release; opening-auction spread and slippage must be frozen later.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: High for a liquid ETF, subject to future cost preregistration; not an execution authorization.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Low symbol-survivorship risk; release-date and next-session mapping must remain point-in-time.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Distinct NEXT_SESSION_RESPONSE hypothesis in family FOMC_SCHEDULED_POLICY_INFORMATION; does not inherit or reopen the frozen exact-time M3 attempt.
cards.result_time_estimate: Resolve a direct mechanism source first, then the XNYS identity; no outcome-bearing run is authorized.

cards.economic_mechanism: Equity risk may be repriced in the hours before a scheduled FOMC announcement as intermediaries reduce uncertainty exposure.
cards.primary_original_source: Lucca and Moench, The Pre-FOMC Announcement Drift, New York Fed Staff Report 512: https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/staff_reports/sr512.html
cards.universe: SPY intraday bars only
cards.signal_and_holding_period: fixed pre-announcement window ending before the official decision timestamp; flatten at the announcement
cards.required_data: official historical FOMC timestamps; split-aware SPY minute bars including session labels; immutable sample export
cards.sample_size_estimate: about eight scheduled meetings per year; a 1994-start identity needs NYSE TAQ rather than the 2003-start flat-file route
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: low event count but time-sensitive fills and spread widening
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: plausible in SPY; no leverage and no execution during the announcement minute
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: low symbol risk; high timestamp-regime risk because decision times and press-conference conventions changed
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: PASS: pre-announcement intraday mechanism is independent of all closed daily allocation lines
cards.result_time_estimate: sample qualification first; 5+ working days after authorization

cards.economic_mechanism: The official FOMC decision resolves policy uncertainty and generates cross-asset price discovery from the announcement through the close.
cards.primary_original_source: Andersen, Bollerslev, Diebold and Vega, Real-Time Price Discovery in Global Stock, Bond and Foreign Exchange Markets: https://www.federalreserve.gov/pubs/ifdp/2006/871/default.htm
cards.universe: SPY, TLT, GLD, and a dollar proxy observed separately; no allocation across them
cards.signal_and_holding_period: official decision timestamp to regular-session close
cards.required_data: official historical decision timestamps; minute bars with UTC and Eastern conversion; RTH flags; corporate actions
cards.sample_size_estimate: about eight events per year with fewer usable same-session closes on exceptional dates
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: low frequency but extreme announcement-minute spread and slippage risk
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: plausible only with liquid ETFs and a no-trade buffer around the release timestamp
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: moderate timestamp and session-regime risk
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: PASS: event reaction measured separately by asset; no GLD safe-haven sleeve or top-one weighting

cards.economic_mechanism: A scheduled inflation release can shift discount-rate and cash-flow expectations even without using an as-released surprise value; test a frozen event-day to next-session window.

cards.universe: liquid broad-equity and intermediate-Treasury ETFs evaluated separately, not dynamically weighted

cards.required_data: official CPI release calendar and timestamps; daily adjusted OHLC; holiday calendar
cards.sample_size_estimate: about 12 releases per year; roughly 195 events over 2010 through mid-2026
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: low monthly turnover; gap risk dominates ordinary spread cost
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: strong in the named liquid ETFs with no leverage
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: low if vintage release dates, postponements, and timezone are frozen from BLS records
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: PASS: scheduled inflation-event mechanism; no prohibited trend, allocation, or GLD safe-haven rule

cards.economic_mechanism: The Employment Situation release resolves labor-growth and policy-rate uncertainty on a scheduled date; test the event window without forecast-surprise inputs.

cards.universe: one broad-equity ETF and one Treasury ETF studied as separate responses
cards.signal_and_holding_period: official NFP release date; prior close to release-day close, with no intraday timestamp dependence
cards.required_data: official BLS Employment Situation calendar; daily adjusted OHLC; exchange calendar
cards.sample_size_estimate: about 12 releases per year; roughly 195 events over 2010 through mid-2026
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: low monthly turnover; scheduled gap exposure requires conservative fills
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: strong in liquid unlevered ETFs
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: low after release-date and rescheduling provenance is frozen
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: PASS: labor-announcement timing is independent of every stated closed line

cards.economic_mechanism: Risk compensation may concentrate on a preregistered union of major scheduled macro-announcement dates rather than on any one indicator.

cards.signal_and_holding_period: fixed union of FOMC, CPI, NFP, PCE, advance GDP, and ISM release dates; prior close to event close
cards.required_data: official Fed, BLS, BEA, and ISM calendars; daily adjusted OHLC; collision and holiday rules

cards.economic_mechanism: Information arriving during the market closure may be priced differently from information arriving during the regular session.

cards.signal_and_holding_period: At monthly decision dates, closeness of the prior close to the trailing 252-session high; long-only top eligible subset for one month, 5-10 equal-risk whole-share positions, cash if fewer qualify.
cards.required_data: Stable security ID, raw/adjusted daily OHLCV, listing/delisting, corporate actions, major-exchange/security-type history and terminal-value policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Expected hundreds to low thousands of eligible firm-months per year after liquidity filters; exact count deferred to outcome-free snapshot qualification.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium; monthly refresh and rank boundary churn require conservative spread/slippage assumptions.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good for 5-10 liquid whole-share positions with cash buffer and no leverage.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until delisted stocks, dated exchange status and adjustment lineage are proven.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Distinct price anchor, but must be frozen so it cannot collapse into the explicitly closed 12-1 momentum or broad-trend line.
cards.implementation_size_estimate: 120-180 lines in one feature/selection adapter after shared data qualification.

cards.economic_mechanism: Industry-level information diffusion and common cash-flow shocks may persist after firm-specific noise is removed.
cards.primary_original_source: Moskowitz and Grinblatt, Do Industries Explain Momentum?, Journal of Finance (1999), https://doi.org/10.1111/0022-1082.00146
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid major-exchange common stocks mapped point in time to coarse SIC industries; 5-10 stocks across leading industries, long only.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Rank industries by a fixed intermediate-horizon past-price aggregate that is preregistered separately from 12-1; hold representative liquid stocks for one month.
cards.required_data: Survivor-aware adjusted daily prices, dated SIC, security identity, corporate actions and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Dozens of industries and hundreds of eligible firm-months per year; exact count deferred.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium; industry re-ranking and within-industry selection can create churn.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Moderate; enforce 5-10 liquid names and industry caps.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High without dated industry and delisting coverage.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Potential overlap with closed momentum/trend is material; only an explicitly industry-defined, non-12-1 preregistration is admissible.

cards.economic_mechanism: Removing common-factor exposures may isolate slower firm-specific information diffusion.
cards.primary_original_source: Blitz, Huij and Martens, Residual Momentum, Journal of Empirical Finance (2011), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jempfin.2011.01.003
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid US common stocks with adequate factor-estimation history.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Predeclared intermediate-horizon cumulative residual signal, long-only strongest residual-continuation names for one month.
cards.required_data: Survivor-aware adjusted prices, benchmark/factor series, security history, corporate actions and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible firm-months annually after history and liquidity requirements.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good if limited to 5-10 liquid names.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High; factor residuals are invalid if security histories are survivor-biased.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Close conceptual neighbor of the closed 12-1 momentum line; park unless Manager confirms independent definition and family allocation.

cards.economic_mechanism: Temporary liquidity imbalance and inventory pressure can reverse after market-wide movement is removed.
cards.primary_original_source: Jegadeesh, Evidence of Predictable Behavior of Security Returns, Journal of Finance (1990), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.1990.tb05110.x; Blitz et al., Short-term residual reversal (2013), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.finmar.2012.10.005
cards.universe: Survivor-aware highly liquid major-exchange common stocks; exclude earnings windows and microcaps.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Five-session market-residual decline, enter after close information is available, hold five sessions; long-only 5-10 names.
cards.required_data: Raw/adjusted daily OHLCV, benchmark daily levels, security identity, corporate actions, trading status and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Potentially thousands of eligible stock-weeks annually, with a small extreme tail selected.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: High; strict liquidity filters and conservative costs are decisive.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Moderate; whole-share capacity is fine but weekly turnover may dominate.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until halted/delisted securities and exact daily eligibility are covered.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent of closed intermediate momentum and broad trend; no outcome evidence checked.

cards.economic_mechanism: Retail attention and opening imbalances can temporarily elevate the open and reverse intraday.
cards.primary_original_source: Berkman, Koch, Tuttle and Zhang, Paying Attention: Overnight Returns and the Hidden Cost of Buying at the Open, JFQA (2012), https://doi.org/10.1017/S0022109012000270
cards.universe: Survivor-aware, highly liquid major-exchange common stocks; exclude earnings and corporate-action gaps.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Large market-residual close-to-open gap; enter only after a fixed post-open delay and exit same day or next close, long-only negative-gap recovery variant.
cards.required_data: Unadjusted open/close, intraday execution proxy, corporate-action and event exclusions, identity and delisting coverage.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Potentially hundreds of liquid gap events annually after exclusions.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Very high; opening spreads and delay slippage may erase economics.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Weak to moderate; only 5-10 very liquid names and conservative opening costs.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High plus event-exclusion PIT risk.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent intraday event mechanism.
cards.result_time_estimate: 6-8 working days after intraday route qualification.

cards.economic_mechanism: Slow diffusion after a new information-driven price range break may persist beyond the breakout date.
cards.primary_original_source: Brock, Lakonishok and LeBaron, Simple Technical Trading Rules and the Stochastic Properties of Stock Returns, Journal of Finance (1992), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.1992.tb04681.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid US common stocks with 252-session history.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Fresh 252-session high confirmed at close, long-only hold a fixed 20 sessions with no parameter grid.
cards.required_data: Survivor-aware daily highs/closes, adjustments, identity, listing history and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible breakouts annually after liquidity/history filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good in 5-10 liquid positions.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until failed issues and split-adjustment semantics are proven.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Broad trend is explicitly closed; this breakout formulation is too adjacent without Manager-approved independence proof.

cards.economic_mechanism: Persistent investor clienteles trading near the open may create same-session-component continuation.
cards.primary_original_source: Lou, Polk and Skouras, A Tug of War: Overnight versus Intraday Expected Returns, JFE (2019), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.03.011
cards.universe: Survivor-aware highly liquid common stocks with reliable unadjusted open and prior close.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Past fixed-window close-to-open component, long-only overnight winners held close-to-next-open with whole-share sizing.
cards.required_data: Point-in-time open/close, corporate-action corrections, identity, trading calendars and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible stock-nights per day before selecting 5-10 names.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Very high daily turnover and opening execution risk.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Weak to moderate because daily whole-share rebalancing and gaps constrain control.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High; split timing and delisted issues can corrupt overnight components.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent session-decomposition mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Opposing retail-at-open and institutional-intraday clienteles may create persistent return decomposition.
cards.primary_original_source: Lou, Polk and Skouras (2019), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.03.011
cards.universe: Survivor-aware top-liquidity US common stocks.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Fixed past-window overnight strength and intraday weakness interaction; hold next overnight only, long-only.
cards.required_data: Reliable open/close histories, corrections, identity and delisting coverage.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible firm-days per year in the joint extreme tail.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Very high.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Weak; daily 5-10 position churn and opening gaps.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent session interaction.

cards.economic_mechanism: Firm-specific overnight information can be separated from broad overnight market movement; residual gaps may distinguish news from market repricing.
cards.primary_original_source: Lou, Polk and Skouras (2019), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2019.03.011
cards.universe: Survivor-aware top-liquidity common stocks.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Prior close to open residual versus market overnight move; fixed long-only continuation or reversal direction must be preregistered from theory before outcomes; hold one session.
cards.required_data: Synchronized unadjusted open/close for stocks and market, corporate actions, identity and delisting coverage.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Thousands of firm-days annually; effective tail count lower.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Very high.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Weak to moderate in only the most liquid names.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent of closed daily trend/momentum.

cards.economic_mechanism: Daily price impact proxies compensation for illiquidity, but for a small account the safest long-only use is to exclude the most costly tail rather than depend on microcaps.
cards.primary_original_source: Amihud, Illiquidity and Stock Returns, Journal of Financial Markets (2002), https://doi.org/10.1016/S1386-4181(01)00024-6
cards.universe: Survivor-aware major-exchange common stocks above a fixed dollar-volume floor.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Monthly Amihud ratio used as an exclusion/quality overlay, then hold 5-10 liquid names selected by an independent mechanism for one month.
cards.required_data: Daily price changes and dollar volume, identity, corporate actions, listing/delisting and terminal policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds to thousands of firm-months annually.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium when used as a slow exclusion filter.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; directly guards against microcap and spread dependence.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until inactive securities and volume histories qualify.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Diagnostic/overlay, not a duplicate of closed momentum or trend.

cards.economic_mechanism: Unusual trading activity can broaden investor recognition and reveal persistent attention demand.
cards.primary_original_source: Gervais, Kaniel and Mingelgrin, The High-Volume Return Premium, Journal of Finance (2001), https://doi.org/10.1111/0022-1082.00349
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid major-exchange common stocks, excluding event and microcap tails.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: One-week volume relative to a fixed trailing baseline; long-only high-attention names for one month.
cards.required_data: Raw volume, split-adjusted shares/price, identity, listing history and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of abnormal-volume events annually after filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium; volume shocks may coincide with spreads and events.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good if minimum dollar-volume and 5-10 position limits apply.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until volume and corporate-action histories are qualified.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent attention mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Turnover can condition the life cycle of price continuation by distinguishing crowded attention from neglected information.
cards.primary_original_source: Lee and Swaminathan, Price Momentum and Trading Volume, Journal of Finance (2000), https://doi.org/10.1111/0022-1082.00280
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with PIT shares outstanding.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Fixed turnover shock relative to trailing baseline used to condition a non-12-1 price-anchor signal; monthly hold.
cards.required_data: Daily volume, PIT shares outstanding, price anchor, identity and delisting history.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-months in extreme turnover states annually.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium to high.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Moderate; select only liquid 5-10 names.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for PIT shares and failed listings.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Conditional overlay risks drifting into closed momentum; keep parked pending independent definition.

cards.economic_mechanism: Preference for lottery-like extreme daily payoffs can create over-demand; a long-only portfolio can exclude the highest-MAX tail.
cards.primary_original_source: Bali, Cakici and Whitelaw, Maxing Out: Stocks as Lotteries and the Cross-Section of Expected Returns, JFE (2011), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2010.08.014
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks; exclude microcaps and OTC.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Maximum daily price change in prior month used only to exclude the lottery tail; monthly low-turnover long-only basket.
cards.required_data: Daily adjusted prices, identity, security type, listing/delisting and corporate actions.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds to thousands of firm-months annually before exclusion.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium as an exclusion filter.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; supports avoiding speculative illiquid names.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until delisted lottery-like failures are included.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent speculative-demand screen.

cards.economic_mechanism: Benchmarking and leverage constraints can cause excess demand for high-risk stocks, leaving low residual-volatility stocks comparatively neglected.
cards.primary_original_source: Baker, Bradley and Wurgler, Benchmarks as Limits to Arbitrage, Financial Analysts Journal (2011), https://doi.org/10.2469/faj.v67.n1.4
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks, sector-balanced, no leverage and no short leg.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Trailing residual volatility estimated on a fixed window; monthly 5-10 lowest-risk eligible names with sector caps.
cards.required_data: Daily prices, benchmark/factors, dated sector, identity, delisting and corporate actions.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible firm-months annually.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; unlevered 5-10 whole-share positions.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High because failed high-risk stocks must remain in the universe even if excluded by the signal.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent defensive mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Investors may price covariance with market downside differently from upside covariance; a long-only low-downside-beta basket targets defensive exposure.
cards.primary_original_source: Ang, Chen and Xing, Downside Risk, Review of Financial Studies (2006), https://doi.org/10.1093/rfs/hhj035
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with sufficient daily history.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Fixed-window downside beta on market-down days; monthly 5-10 lowest-downside-beta names, sector capped.
cards.required_data: Daily prices and benchmark, PIT sector, identity, corporate actions and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-months; estimation needs enough down-market observations.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High until full failed-stock histories qualify.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent risk mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Profitability and balance-sheet safety may screen out distressed low-beta traps while retaining the benchmark-constraint low-risk prior.
cards.primary_original_source: Asness, Frazzini and Pedersen, Quality Minus Junk, Review of Accounting Studies (2019), https://doi.org/10.1007/s11142-018-9470-2; Baker et al. (2011), https://doi.org/10.2469/faj.v67.n1.4
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with accession-timestamped SEC fundamentals.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Fixed quality screen then lowest-beta subset; quarterly or monthly 5-10 long-only positions.
cards.required_data: Survivor-aware prices, PIT SEC facts, accepted timestamps, security mapping, identity and delisting policy.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-quarters after six-year PIT history and liquidity filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high until accession-bound fundamentals join a permanent security key.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent composite, but must not become an unconstrained parameter blend.
cards.result_time_estimate: 6-8 working days after both snapshots qualify.

cards.economic_mechanism: A split can convey management confidence and alter salience/retail accessibility; any slow response must be tested after the public event.
cards.primary_original_source: Ikenberry, Rankine and Stice, What Do Stock Splits Really Signal?, JFQA (1996), https://doi.org/10.2307/2331396
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with ordinary forward splits and no simultaneous merger or special distribution.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Enter after split announcement acceptance/public timestamp, hold one month; separate announcement from effective date.
cards.required_data: Split announcement/effective dates, SEC/exchange source documents, raw/adjusted prices, identity and delisting history.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Dozens to low hundreds of clean split events annually.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for announcement-time identity and adjustment handling.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent corporate-action event.

cards.economic_mechanism: Investors may underreact to earnings innovations relative to the issuer's own seasonal history, creating delayed incorporation after public filing.
cards.primary_original_source: Bernard and Thomas, Evidence that Stock Prices Do Not Fully Reflect the Implications of Current Earnings for Future Earnings, JAE (1990), https://doi.org/10.1016/0165-4101(90)90008-R
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid US common stocks with at least eight PIT quarterly observations and unambiguous CIK-to-security mapping.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Seasonal time-series earnings surprise from as-filed values, formed only after accepted timestamp; next-session entry and fixed 20-session hold, long-only positive surprises.
cards.required_data: CIK, accession, form, accepted timestamp, period end, fiscal period, as-filed EPS or income/share inputs, unit, source-document hash, amendment lineage, permanent security ID and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Expected thousands of issuer-quarter observations over six years before liquidity/history filters; exact count deferred.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium; quarterly events but clustered announcements and gaps.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good for 5-10 liquid names with next-session execution and cash.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high until accession-level facts and permanent security mapping are qualified; amendments must not overwrite original availability.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent earnings-event mechanism based on authorized control files only.
cards.result_time_estimate: 6-8 working days after SEC and price snapshots qualify.

cards.economic_mechanism: Revenue innovations contain demand information not fully captured by earnings and may diffuse slowly.
cards.primary_original_source: Jegadeesh and Livnat, Revenue Surprises and Stock Returns, JAE (2006), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jacceco.2005.10.003
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid US common stocks with consistent PIT quarterly revenue tags.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Seasonal revenue surprise against own history; next-session entry after accepted filing, hold 20 sessions, long-only positive surprises.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound revenue facts, accepted timestamp, fiscal metadata, units, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Thousands of issuer-quarters over six years, reduced by tag consistency and liquidity filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for tag normalization, amendments and security mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent top-line event signal.

cards.economic_mechanism: Unexpected reporting delay can signal operational or accounting difficulty before the filing content is processed.
cards.primary_original_source: Chambers and Penman, Timeliness of Reporting and the Stock Price Reaction to Earnings Announcements, JAR (1984), https://doi.org/10.2307/2490700
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with sufficient own-history filing calendars.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Deviation of accepted filing date from issuer's seasonal historical cadence, using only information known by the expected date; long-only avoidance/cash overlay until filing.
cards.required_data: CIK/accession/form/accepted timestamp, fiscal period, historical cadence, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of late-filing issuer-quarters over six years after a minimum own-history rule.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low as an avoidance overlay.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; cash allowed.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High because expected-date models must use only prior filings and avoid hindsight.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent filing-timing mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Systematic information arrival and investor attention around scheduled earnings dates may create a distinct event-period premium.
cards.primary_original_source: Frazzini and Lamont, The Earnings Announcement Premium and Trading Volume, NBER Working Paper 13090 (2007), https://www.nber.org/papers/w13090
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with expected earnings date inferred only from prior public cadence or official notice.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Hold a fixed window around a causally known/estimated announcement date, with no use of future actual date; 5-10 names and cash.
cards.required_data: PIT expected-date rule, accepted timestamps, historical cadence, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Thousands of firm-quarter events over six years, but expected-date qualification lowers usable count.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Medium to high due gaps and clustering.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Moderate; overlapping announcements can exceed 10 positions.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for expected-date look-ahead and rescheduling.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent scheduled-event mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Repurchase authorization can signal perceived undervaluation, but authorization is not execution and must remain distinct from buyback completion.
cards.primary_original_source: Ikenberry, Lakonishok and Vermaelen, Market Underreaction to Open Market Share Repurchases, JFE (1995), https://doi.org/10.1016/0304-405X(95)00826-Z
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with official 8-K/10-Q/10-K repurchase authorization disclosure.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Enter next session after accession-verified new authorization; fixed one-month hold, excluding renewals and simultaneous earnings surprises under a frozen rule.
cards.required_data: Accession, accepted timestamp, exhibit/text-light authorization flag, prior authorization history, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds to low thousands of distinct authorizations over six years before de-duplication.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for new-versus-repeat classification and issuer/security mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent corporate payout event.

cards.economic_mechanism: A new recurring cash payout can signal durable cash-flow confidence and may be incorporated gradually.
cards.primary_original_source: Michaely, Thaler and Womack, Price Reactions to Dividend Initiations and Omissions, Journal of Finance (1995), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.1995.tb04796.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid dividend-paying common stocks with at least eight prior quarters of PIT distribution history.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: First regular cash dividend after a fixed no-dividend lookback, recognized after public filing/announcement; next-session entry, one-month hold.
cards.required_data: Official dividend declaration, accepted/public timestamp, amount/type/ex-date, prior history, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Low hundreds of clean initiations over six years.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for complete prior distribution history and security mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent payout event.

cards.economic_mechanism: Dividend cuts or omissions can reveal deterioration that may diffuse slowly; a long-only process can move to cash rather than short.
cards.primary_original_source: Michaely, Thaler and Womack (1995), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.1995.tb04796.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with complete PIT dividend histories.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Exclude for a fixed period after a publicly announced material regular-dividend cut or omission.
cards.required_data: Declaration timestamps, dividend type/amount/frequency, prior history, identity and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of cuts/omissions over six years including stress periods.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low as an exclusion overlay.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; cash allowed.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High because omitted distributions and distressed delistings correlate with outcomes.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent payout-risk overlay.

cards.economic_mechanism: Seasoned equity issuance may reflect financing pressure or managerial timing; long-only portfolios can exclude recent issuers.
cards.primary_original_source: Loughran and Ritter, The New Issues Puzzle, Journal of Finance (1995), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.1995.tb05166.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with SEC offering and effectiveness identity.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Exclude for a fixed period after completed common-stock SEO, starting after effectiveness/closing is public.
cards.required_data: Registration accession, effectiveness notice, prospectus, offering/closing date, share class, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of SEOs over six years after common-stock and completion filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low as an exclusion overlay.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for completion timing and share-class mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent financing-event screen.

cards.economic_mechanism: Cumulative share issuance versus contraction summarizes financing and payout choices that may be incompletely priced.
cards.primary_original_source: Pontiff and Woodgate, Share Issuance and Cross-Sectional Returns, Journal of Finance (2008), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.2008.01335.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with PIT diluted/basic shares and split histories.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Annual split-adjusted net share issuance from as-filed values, lagged to accepted timestamp; long-only low-issuance/share-contraction names for one quarter.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound shares outstanding/issuance/repurchase facts, split lineage, accepted timestamps, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-years over six years after tag-quality and liquidity filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low quarterly/annual turnover.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for share-class actions, restatements and split-adjusted as-filed interpretation.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent financing-policy mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Multiple independent insiders buying in a short public window can aggregate private assessments and reduce idiosyncratic motives.
cards.primary_original_source: Lakonishok and Lee, Are Insider Trades Informative?, Review of Financial Studies (2001), https://doi.org/10.1093/rfs/14.1.79; Cohen, Malloy and Pomorski (2012), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.2012.01740.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with as-filed Forms 4/5 and identified officers/directors.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: At least two distinct insiders reporting open-market purchases within a frozen short window; enter next session after the final accepted filing, hold one month.
cards.required_data: Issuer CIK, reporting owner, role, accession, accepted/filed time, transaction date/code, direct/indirect ownership, shares/price, 10b5-1 flag, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of cluster events over six years after open-market and role filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low to medium.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for owner deduplication, amendments, transaction codes and share-class mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent insider event.

cards.economic_mechanism: The delay between transaction and public filing may condition information decay and disclosure behavior.
cards.primary_original_source: Brochet, Information Content of Insider Trades before and after the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, The Accounting Review (2010), https://doi.org/10.2308/accr.2010.85.2.419
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with exact transaction and accepted timestamps.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Open-market purchase classified by transaction-to-acceptance lag; trade only after filing acceptance, hold one month.
cards.required_data: Transaction date, accepted timestamp, accession, owner identity/role, code, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Thousands of purchase filings over six years; lag tails provide hundreds of events.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for causal timing; transaction date itself was not public before filing.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Conditioning variant of insider purchases; park behind cluster card.

cards.economic_mechanism: Gross profits relative to assets capture productive efficiency before many discretionary expenses and may complement value.
cards.primary_original_source: Novy-Marx, The Other Side of Value: The Gross Profitability Premium, JFE (2013), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2013.01.003
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid nonfinancial common stocks with consistent PIT annual facts.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: As-filed gross profit divided by prior-period total assets, available only after acceptance; quarterly refresh, 5-10 highest-quality eligible names with sector caps.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound revenue/cost-of-revenue or gross-profit and assets, accepted timestamps, units, fiscal periods, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of eligible firm-years over six years after financial-sector and tag-consistency filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for XBRL tag normalization, restatements and permanent security mapping.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent quality mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Rapid balance-sheet expansion can reflect overinvestment or extrapolative financing, while restrained investment may preserve returns on capital.
cards.primary_original_source: Cooper, Gulen and Schill, Asset Growth and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns, Journal of Finance (2008), https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-6261.2008.01370.x
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid nonfinancial common stocks with two consecutive PIT annual asset facts.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Year-over-year total asset growth using first as-filed values, lagged to later filing acceptance; quarterly refresh, long-only lowest-growth eligible names.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound assets for consecutive periods, accepted timestamps, amendments, units, fiscal metadata, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-years over six years after history and liquidity filters.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for first-filed values, acquisition accounting, restatements and dead firms.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent investment mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Investors may over-weight less persistent accrual components relative to cash-flow components of earnings.
cards.primary_original_source: Sloan, Do Stock Prices Fully Reflect Information in Accruals and Cash Flows About Future Earnings?, The Accounting Review (1996), https://doi.org/10.2308/tar-9608042309
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid nonfinancial common stocks with complete PIT balance-sheet and cash-flow facts.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Frozen balance-sheet or cash-flow accrual definition from first as-filed annual data; quarterly refresh, long-only low-accrual names.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound income, cash flow and balance-sheet facts, accepted timestamps, units, fiscal metadata, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-years over six years; complete-tag requirement reduces coverage.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high; taxonomy changes and restatements can silently contaminate PIT values.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent accounting-quality mechanism.

cards.economic_mechanism: Within inexpensive stocks, profitability, leverage, liquidity and operating improvement can distinguish financially sound firms from distress traps.
cards.primary_original_source: Piotroski, Value Investing: The Use of Historical Financial Statement Information to Separate Winners from Losers, JAR (2000), https://doi.org/10.2307/2672906
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid nonfinancial common stocks with complete PIT annual statements and market value after filing.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Predeclared compact subset of accounting safety indicators plus book-to-market, all from first as-filed data; quarterly refresh, 5-10 long-only names.
cards.required_data: Accession-bound profitability, cash flow, leverage, liquidity, shares and assets/equity, accepted timestamps, amendments, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of firm-years over six years, but complete-field and 5-10 position constraints may concentrate selection.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good if microcaps are excluded and sector concentration capped.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: Very high for multi-field completeness, as-filed timestamps and share-class market value.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Composite risks hidden multiplicity; only one frozen compact specification is admissible.

cards.economic_mechanism: A disclosed material weakness in internal control can signal reporting reliability and operational risk; long-only portfolios can move to cash or exclude the issuer.
cards.primary_original_source: Doyle, Ge and McVay, Determinants of Weaknesses in Internal Control over Financial Reporting, JAE (2007), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jacceco.2006.10.003
cards.universe: Survivor-aware liquid common stocks with 10-K/10-Q internal-control disclosures.
cards.signal_and_holding_period: Deterministic phrase and section rule on the as-filed document, activated after accepted timestamp; exclude for a fixed period until a later clean filing.
cards.required_data: Accession, form, accepted timestamp, source-document hash, amendment lineage, deterministic disclosure extraction, security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
cards.sample_size_estimate: Hundreds of disclosures over six years, but exact deterministic coverage is unverified.
cards.turnover_and_cost_risk: Low as an exclusion overlay.
cards.USD_40000_execution_fit: Good; cash allowed.
cards.survivorship_and_PIT_risk: High for document-version and amended-disclosure handling.
cards.closed_lineage_duplicate_screen: Independent filing-metadata risk screen.
cards.implementation_size_estimate: Over 300 lines if generalized; defer to avoid creating an NLP/text platform.
cards.result_time_estimate: Deferred until a bounded deterministic extractor is separately authorized.

2.criterion: Exact source with material implementation translation, or strong economic prior only.

3.criterion: Exact mechanism source plus at least one independent supporting source.

1.criterion: Paid source, multi-system mapping, or major unresolved coverage remains.

2.criterion: One bounded official-identity or join task remains.

3.criterion: All required identities are qualified locally and immutable.

1.criterion: Expected count is marginal or highly clustered.

2.criterion: Expected count clearly exceeds the frozen minimum across enough years.

1.criterion: Material concentration, turnover, or gap constraints.

2.criterion: Low turnover or large event prior relative to conservative costs.

1.criterion: Adjacent to a closed line and requires an explicit guard.

0.criterion: New platform, NLP stack, optimizer, or high-complexity engine needed.
Future evidence is not practically collectable.

1.criterion: Future observations are slow or sparse.

2.criterion: Frequent and objectively timestamped future observations.

total.calculation: sum of the eight dimension scores

readiness_barriers.class: Lane B daily (excluded from source qualification priorities)
readiness_barriers.reason: M3 is PARK_SOURCE_MISMATCH_AND_XNYS_INCOMPLETE; resolve a direct mechanism source before XNYS identity, and do not reopen exact-time M3.

readiness_barriers.class: existing-data daily ETF or broad market
readiness_barriers.reason: Required daily input identities were not qualified without prohibited price/database access.
No-purchase survivor-aware sample and terminal-value contract not passed.
Accession PIT data still lacks qualified permanent security mapping and survivor-aware prices.
Explicit user purchase authorization absent; spend ceiling is USD 0.

formal_mechanism_decision.narrowest_next_action: Submit this semantic delta for external review; any later source qualification remains outcome-blind and creates no strategy candidate.

unresolved_evidence_gaps.gap: No mechanism-specific original paper was found for an equal-weight minus cap-weight ETF spread as a broad-versus-concentrated participation state; index mathematics is operational documentation only.
unresolved_evidence_gaps.required_action: Run only a bounded mechanism-specific source qualification task or keep PARKED.

unresolved_evidence_gaps.gap: No price, return, database, NAV, Sharpe, validation, or holdout object was opened; operational routes remain unqualified.
No provider purchase authorization; spend ceiling remains USD 0.
Diagnostic estimator, lookback, initialization, shock threshold, provenance, decay estimator, and decay-exit specifications are absent.
unresolved_evidence_gaps.required_action: Freeze those diagnostic specifications before prospective observation; do not infer a SPY-to-cash, risk-off, alpha, or net risk-adjusted-return claim.
